mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals an improving labor market, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, thereby supporting corporate earnings and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or UNRATE decline does not correlate with positive SPX returns in > 55% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above a critical threshold, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy stance. This increases the cost of capital and discount rates for future earnings, putting downward pressure on equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the average return in such periods is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in retail sales (RSAFS) combined with strong housing starts (HOUST) indicates broad-based consumer and investment demand. This joint strength drives corporate revenues and profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND HOUST 3-month average growth > 2.0%
failure_condition: Joint strength in RSAFS and HOUST does not consistently lead to positive SPX returns, with a Rank IC below 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) growth during a post-recession recovery signals renewed manufacturing activity and capacity utilization. This translates into improved corporate profitability and higher equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration during recovery periods does not consistently precede positive SPX returns, or average returns are not significantly positive (p-value > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects a healthy and expanding economy. This fundamental strength typically translates into higher corporate revenues and profits across sectors, supporting overall equity market appreciation over longer horizons.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth does not correlate with positive SPX returns over a 60-day horizon, with a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant monthly increase in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates robust job creation and a tightening labor market. This boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to immediate positive sentiment and S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite a positive PAYEMS signal, or average returns are not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) suggests an overly accommodative monetary policy despite rising prices. This can fuel asset speculation and lead to short-term positive equity returns as real rates are suppressed.
failure_condition: SPX fails to show positive 20d returns when CPIAUCSL is high and FEDFUNDS is low, with an average return below 0% in such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Weak retail sales (RSAFS) combined with high unemployment (UNRATE) signals a severe contraction in consumer demand and economic activity. This indicates poor corporate prospects and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite negative signal during recessionary periods, or the average return is not significantly negative.
